name: Pure Recovery Jog
description: Z1 only. The sole purpose is blood flow to flush the legs after a hard session. No strides, no pickups, no acceleration. If you feel the urge to speed up, slow down.

main_set.description: Very easy jog at Z1 only. Slower than your easy pace. Comfortable nasal breathing throughout.

cooldown.description: 3 min walk + foam roll quads, calves, glutes

name: Shakeout Recovery Run
description: Short, gentle shakeout the day after a hard session. Shorter than Pure Recovery — for days you're still stiff or if the long run was especially demanding.

warmup.description: 5 min walk — let the body wake up before running

main_set.description: Easy jog at Z1. 10–20 min total. Walk breaks fine if you need them.

name: Recovery Walk-Jog
description: Alternates easy jog and walk. For beginner runners or days when even Z1 jogging feels too much. Movement without stress.

main_set.description: Alternate 3 min easy jog + 1 min walk. Z1 throughout.

name: Recovery Spin
description: Z1 only. Active recovery on the bike — legs spinning freely, no resistance, no power targets. Blood flow without fatigue.

warmup.description: 5 min very easy spin — settle in

main_set.description: Easy spin at Z1. 20–35 min. If you feel yourself pushing, back off.

cooldown.description: 5 min easy spin → off the bike, stretch quads and hip flexors

name: Recovery Flush Swim
description: Gentle swim focused on easy stroke mechanics and blood flow. No pace targets, no hard strokes. Flush the legs after a land-based hard session.

main_set.description: Easy continuous swim at Z1. Smooth, relaxed stroke. Mix freestyle and backstroke if you like.

name: Steady Easy Run
description: Classic easy effort. Conversational pace throughout. The bread and butter.

warmup.description: Start with 3–5 min walk/very easy jog to settle in

main_set.description: Steady easy effort — stay conversational the entire time

name: Easy Run with Strides
description: Easy effort with short accelerations at the end to keep legs snappy without adding fatigue.

main_set.description: Easy run + finishing strides

blocks.description: Easy aerobic run
Strides: smooth acceleration to near-sprint, focus on form not effort

name: Easy Progression Run
description: Start very easy, finish at the upper end of easy. Teaches pace discipline and negative splitting.

warmup.description: 5 min very easy jog (slower than Z2)

main_set.description: Negative split easy run — each third slightly faster but all within Z1–Z2

blocks.description: First third: very easy, lower Z1
Middle third: settle into Z2
Final third: upper Z2, still comfortable

name: Walk-Run Easy Session
description: Alternating walk and run intervals. Perfect for new runners building aerobic base safely.

main_set.description: Alternating run and walk — run should feel easy, walk is active recovery

name: Steady State Tempo
description: Continuous tempo effort. The classic threshold builder — sustained, controlled, not a race.

main_set.description: Sustained tempo — lock into Z3 and hold. Should feel comfortably hard.

name: Cruise Intervals
description: Tempo broken into repeats with short rest. Same threshold stimulus, more manageable for longer total time at Z3.

main_set.description: Cruise intervals at threshold — the short rest keeps HR elevated while giving a mental break

name: Tempo with Surge Finish
description: Sustained tempo with a short harder pickup at the end. Teaches finishing under fatigue.

warmup.description: 12 min easy jog + dynamic drills + 4×100m strides

main_set.description: Sustained tempo with a surge to practice closing under fatigue

blocks.description: Steady tempo — patient, controlled
Surge to close — pick it up to Z4 for the final minutes

name: Progression Tempo
description: Start at lower tempo, build to upper threshold. Teaches the feel of progressive pacing on tired legs.

main_set.description: Progressive tempo — each segment slightly faster but all controlled

blocks.description: Low Z3 — the easiest tempo pace
Mid Z3 — settle in
Upper Z3 to low Z4 — strong finish

name: Gentle Tempo
description: Conservative tempo for just-finish athletes. Lower Z3, shorter duration. Getting comfortable being uncomfortable.

main_set.description: Gentle tempo — low Z3, should feel like you're working but not suffering

name: Broken Tempo
description: Tempo in shorter chunks with generous recovery. Good stepping stone before sustained tempo.

main_set.description: Short tempo blocks with real recovery — build confidence at threshold

name: Mile Repeat Tempo
description: Classic mile repeats at threshold. Great benchmark workout — track progress over weeks.

main_set.description: Mile repeats — lock into Z3 pace per mile, use the rest to reset mentally

name: Half Marathon Pace Tempo
description: Extended tempo at half marathon race pace. The key session for half and full marathoners.

main_set.description: Extended half marathon pace effort — practice the exact race feel

name: Classic 1K Repeats
description: The gold standard VO2max workout. 1000m repeats at Z4 with equal jog recovery.

main_set.description: 1K repeats — hard but controlled, don't sprint the first one

name: 800m Repeats
description: Shorter, slightly faster VO2max work. Great for 5K and 10K specificity.

main_set.description: 800m repeats — quicker turnover than 1Ks, practice holding form when legs burn

name: 3-Minute VO2max Intervals
description: Time-based VO2max work. Easier to execute than distance-based if no track available.

main_set.description: 3 min hard intervals — long enough to reach VO2max, short enough to maintain quality

name: Ascending Ladder
description: Build from short to long intervals. Mentally easier — the hardest rep is when you're freshest.

main_set.description: Ascending ladder — build into the workout, each rep longer but same effort
main_set.rest_between: equal to work duration, easy jog

name: Pyramid Intervals
description: Up and back down. The longest rep in the middle is the crux — everything after is a victory lap.

main_set.description: Pyramid — build up, hit the peak, come back down. Match the effort on the way down.

name: 400m Speed Repeats
description: Short, fast repeats for neuromuscular power. Sharpening workout for 5K racers.

main_set.description: 400m repeats — fast and crisp, not all-out sprints. Smooth, quick turnover.

name: Long VO2max Intervals
description: 4–5 min repeats for sustained VO2max stimulus. Brutal but effective for half marathoners and above.

main_set.description: Long VO2max — stay at Z4 effort, don't start too fast. These are about time at intensity.

name: Hill Repeats
description: VO2max stimulus with lower impact. The hill provides resistance — no need to go as fast.

main_set.description: Hill repeats — 4–6% grade, strong effort uphill, easy jog down. Focus on driving arms and knees.

name: Steady Long Run
description: Classic long run. Easy pace throughout. Building aerobic endurance and mental toughness.

warmup.description: First 10 min at very easy pace — let the body settle in

main_set.description: Steady Z2 effort — resist the urge to speed up. Practice patience.

name: Long Run with Race-Pace Finish
description: Easy long run with the last 20–30% at race pace. Teaches closing strong on tired legs.

main_set.description: Build into race pace — patient start, strong finish

blocks.description: Easy aerobic — bank the miles
Drop to race pace for the final segment — practice running fast on tired legs

name: Long Run with Mid-Run Tempo
description: Sandwich a tempo block in the middle of a long run. Simulates surging mid-race.

main_set.description: Tempo sandwich — easy start, tempo in the middle, easy to finish

blocks.description: Easy warmup miles
Tempo block — controlled, strong
Easy cool down miles — resist temptation to push

name: Marathon Pace Long Run
description: Extended marathon pace effort embedded in a long run. The key marathon-specific workout.

main_set.description: Extended marathon pace — this is THE workout that builds marathon confidence

blocks.description: Easy to settle in
Marathon pace — steady, sustainable, practice nutrition
Easy to cool down

name: Steady Z2 Ride
description: Classic aerobic endurance ride. Flat or rolling terrain, steady effort, conversational.

warmup.description: 5 min easy spin, gradually increase cadence

main_set.description: Steady Z2 effort — keep cadence 85–95 RPM, controlled breathing

name: Cadence Focus Ride
description: Z2 ride with cadence drills. Build pedaling efficiency and neuromuscular coordination.

main_set.description: Z2 ride with cadence variations — same power, different leg speed

blocks.description: Normal cadence (85–95 RPM)
High cadence spins — smooth pedal circles, no bouncing
Normal cadence to finish

name: Progressive Endurance Ride
description: Start easy, finish at upper Z2. Teaches pacing discipline — don't go out too hard.

main_set.description: Gradual build — each block slightly higher power but all Z2

blocks.description: Low Z1–Z2 — easy spinning
Mid Z2 — steady state
Upper Z2 — strong but sustainable

name: Sweet Spot Intervals
description: 88–94% of FTP — the sweet spot between tempo and threshold. Maximum training stimulus with manageable fatigue.

main_set.description: Sweet spot intervals — feels hard but manageable. You should NOT be gasping.

name: Over-Under Intervals
description: Alternate between just below and just above FTP. Teaches the body to clear lactate while riding hard.

warmup.description: 12 min easy spin + 3×1 min progressive spin-ups

main_set.description: Over-unders — 3 min at 95% FTP (under), 1 min at 105% FTP (over), repeat without stopping. That's 1 block.

intervals.duration: 12 min (3 cycles of 3 min under + 1 min over)

name: FTP Sustained Intervals
description: Long intervals at or near FTP. The purest threshold builder on the bike.

main_set.description: Sustained FTP intervals — right at threshold. These should feel like controlled suffering.

name: Tempo Ride
description: Sustained moderate effort. Builds muscular endurance without the fatigue of threshold work.

main_set.description: Sustained tempo — moderate effort, you can still talk but in shorter sentences

name: VO2max Bike Intervals
description: Short, hard intervals above FTP. Builds peak cycling power and oxygen uptake.

main_set.description: VO2max intervals — above FTP, breathing hard but controlled

name: Steady Long Ride
description: Classic long aerobic ride. Build time in the saddle and endurance foundation.

warmup.description: First 15 min easy spin to settle in

main_set.description: Long steady Z2 — practice fueling every 30–45 min, stay hydrated

name: Long Ride with Race Pace Blocks
description: Long ride with periodic surges to race effort. Simulates race conditions and nutrition strategy.

main_set.description: Long ride with race-pace insertions — practice holding power when fatigued

blocks.description: Easy to settle in
Race-pace surges embedded in the ride — practice fueling during easy segments
Easy to finish — ride home spin

name: Catch and Pull Focus
description: Drill-heavy session focused on the catch phase. Where most swimmers lose free speed.

main_set.description: Catch drills with swimming to apply the feel immediately

blocks.description: Fingertip drag drill — exaggerate high elbow recovery
Swim — focus on applying the high-elbow catch you just drilled
Catch-up drill — one arm completes full stroke before other begins
Swim — blend the catch timing into normal stroke

name: Body Position and Rotation Drills
description: Side drills and rotation work. Fixes the #1 beginner problem: swimming flat.

main_set.description: Rotation and body position drills

blocks.description: Side kick drill — lay on side, bottom arm extended, kick for 25m, rotate and switch
6-3-6 drill — 6 kicks on side, 3 strokes, 6 kicks other side. Forces rotation.
Swim — focus on rotating hips and shoulders together

name: Kick and Breathing Technique
description: Bilateral breathing practice and kick efficiency. Two skills that pay dividends in open water.

main_set.description: Kick and breathing pattern work

blocks.description: Kick with board — streamlined body, small fast kicks from hips not knees
Bilateral breathing drill — breathe every 3 strokes, both sides
Swim — alternate 25m breathing left / 25m breathing right

name: CSS 200s
description: Classic CSS threshold set at 200m intervals. The workhorse triathlon swim workout.

main_set.description: 200m repeats at CSS pace — should feel hard but sustainable. Hold the same split every rep.

name: CSS Descending Set
description: Mix of distances at CSS, getting shorter. More rest as distances drop, so you hold pace even when fatigued.

main_set.description: Descending distances at CSS — maintain the same pace per 100m throughout

blocks.description: 300s at CSS — long and steady
200s at CSS — hold the same split
100s at CSS or slightly faster — finish strong

name: CSS 100s
description: Shorter CSS intervals with less rest. Higher perceived effort, great for race-pace sharpening.

main_set.description: 100m repeats at CSS — short rest keeps HR up. Focus on consistent splits.

name: Broken 400s at CSS
description: 4×100 with 5 sec rest = 1 broken 400. Teaches race pacing over longer distances.

main_set.description: Broken 400s — swim 4×100 with micro-rest, then full recovery between 400s

blocks.description: Each 100 at CSS pace. The 5s rest lets you check your split without breaking rhythm.

name: Race-Pace Sprint Set
description: Short fast 50s above CSS. Neuromuscular speed work for the swim — leg speed and turnover.

main_set.description: Fast 50s — above CSS pace, focus on high turnover and strong kick

name: Steady Swim Endurance
description: Continuous swimming at easy effort. Build comfort in the water and aerobic base.

main_set.description: Continuous easy swimming — focus on rhythm and breathing, not speed

name: Pull Set Endurance
description: Pull buoy between legs, paddles optional. Isolates upper body for endurance with less kick fatigue.

main_set.description: Pull-focused endurance — great for building swim-specific upper body stamina

blocks.description: Pull buoy in, focus on high elbow catch and body rotation
Full stroke — feel the difference with kick added back in

name: Swim Endurance Pyramid
description: Building and descending distances. Mentally engaging way to accumulate volume.

main_set.description: Distance pyramid — build up then come back down. All Z2.

name: Runner's Prehab Circuit
description: Hip, glute, and ankle stability. The non-negotiable circuit for every runner.

warmup.description: 5 min easy walk or jog + dynamic leg swings

main_set.description: Injury prevention circuit — controlled movement, full range of motion

exercises.name: Single-Leg Glute Bridge
exercises.notes: Squeeze at top for 2 sec

exercises.name: Clamshell
exercises.notes: Keep hips stacked, don't roll back

exercises.name: Dead Bug
exercises.notes: Press low back into floor throughout

exercises.name: Single-Leg RDL
exercises.notes: Slow and controlled — balance is the point

exercises.name: Banded Lateral Walk
exercises.notes: Stay low in a quarter squat

name: Core Anti-Rotation Circuit
description: Core stability focused on anti-rotation and anti-extension. Protects the spine during running and cycling.

main_set.description: Core stability — resist rotation and extension. Quality reps, not speed.

exercises.name: Pallof Press
exercises.notes: Arms extend straight, resist the pull to rotate

exercises.name: Plank with Shoulder Tap
exercises.notes: Hips stay level — don't rock side to side

exercises.name: Bird Dog
exercises.notes: Extend opposite arm and leg, hold 2 sec

exercises.name: Side Plank with Hip Dip
exercises.notes: Control the dip — don't crash to the floor

name: Swimmer's Shoulder Prehab
description: Rotator cuff and scapular stability. Prevents the most common triathlete injury: shoulder impingement.

main_set.description: Shoulder stability — light load, controlled tempo, full range

exercises.name: Band External Rotation
exercises.notes: Elbow pinned to side, slow rotation out

exercises.name: Band Pull-Apart
exercises.notes: Squeeze shoulder blades together

exercises.name: Y-T-W Raises
exercises.notes: Face down on bench or standing bent over

exercises.name: Face Pull
exercises.notes: Pull to face height, externally rotate at end

exercises.name: Scapular Push-Up
exercises.notes: Arms locked — movement comes only from shoulder blades

cooldown.description: 2 min doorway chest stretch + cross-body shoulder stretch

name: Runner's Power Session
description: Single-leg strength and hip power for runners. The key lifts that translate to faster running.

warmup.description: 5 min easy jog + leg swings + bodyweight squats

main_set.description: Running-specific power — heavy compound movements, low reps, full recovery

exercises.name: Bulgarian Split Squat
exercises.notes: Full depth, drive through front heel

exercises.name: Single-Leg RDL
exercises.notes: Hip hinge — feel the hamstring stretch

exercises.name: Step-Up with Drive
exercises.notes: Explode up, drive opposite knee high

exercises.name: Calf Raise (single-leg)
exercises.notes: Full range — below parallel at bottom, max squeeze at top

name: Cyclist Power Session
description: Quad and glute dominant. Heavy compounds that build pedal force and climbing power.

warmup.description: 5 min dynamic warmup: hip circles, leg swings, bodyweight squats, arm circles

main_set.description: Cycling-specific power — build force production through pedal stroke range of motion

exercises.name: Barbell Back Squat
exercises.notes: Parallel depth minimum, controlled descent

exercises.name: Leg Press
exercises.notes: Feet shoulder width, full range

exercises.name: Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Hamstring and glute focus — hip hinge pattern

exercises.name: Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Quad isolation — hold at top for 1 sec

exercises.name: Weighted Plank
exercises.notes: Core stiffness for power transfer on the bike

name: Swimmer's Lat and Pull Power
description: Lat, back, and rotational power for faster pulling. The swim-specific strength session.

main_set.description: Swim-specific pulling power — lat dominance, scapular control

exercises.name: Lat Pulldown
exercises.notes: Wide grip, squeeze lats at bottom, control the return

exercises.name: Single-Arm Cable Row
exercises.notes: Rotate slightly toward pulling arm — mimics swim catch

exercises.name: Straight-Arm Pulldown
exercises.notes: Swim-specific catch pattern — feel the lats engage

exercises.name: Face Pull
exercises.notes: Rear delt and external rotation — prevents shoulder impingement

exercises.name: Cable Woodchop
exercises.notes: Rotational power — core transfers force between upper and lower body

cooldown.description: 3 min lat stretch + doorway pec stretch

name: Push Day (Hypertrophy)
description: Chest, shoulders, triceps. Traditional push hypertrophy for the hybrid athlete.

warmup.description: 5 min light cardio + band pull-aparts + arm circles

main_set.description: Push hypertrophy — moderate-heavy weight, controlled tempo, chase the pump

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Full range of motion, 2 sec negative

exercises.name: Incline Dumbbell Press
exercises.notes: 30° incline, upper chest focus

exercises.name: Overhead Press
exercises.notes: Dumbbells or barbell, strict form

exercises.name: Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Control the negative — don't swing

exercises.name: Cable Tricep Pushdown
exercises.notes: Squeeze at bottom, slow return

exercises.name: Dips or Close-Grip Push-Up
exercises.notes: Tricep emphasis — elbows close to body

name: Pull Day (Hypertrophy)
description: Back, biceps, rear delts. Traditional pull hypertrophy. Pairs well with swim day.

main_set.description: Pull hypertrophy — focus on the squeeze, feel the back working

exercises.name: Lat Pulldown
exercises.notes: Wide grip, pull to upper chest

exercises.name: Seated Cable Row
exercises.notes: Squeeze shoulder blades at peak contraction

exercises.name: Dumbbell Row
exercises.notes: Support on bench, full stretch to full squeeze

exercises.name: Face Pull
exercises.notes: Rear delt and external rotation — pull to face, spread hands

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bicep Curl
exercises.notes: No swinging — control both directions

exercises.name: Hammer Curl
exercises.notes: Neutral grip — hits brachialis and forearms

name: Leg Day (Hypertrophy)
description: Quads, glutes, hamstrings. Full leg hypertrophy. Place on swim day or rest day to protect run/bike.

warmup.description: 5 min dynamic warmup: bodyweight squats, leg swings, walking lunges, glute bridges

main_set.description: Leg hypertrophy — moderate-heavy load, chase volume and metabolic stress

exercises.name: Barbell Back Squat or Goblet Squat
exercises.notes: Full depth if mobility allows

exercises.name: Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Feel the hamstring stretch, squeeze glutes at top

exercises.name: Walking Lunge
exercises.notes: Long stride, upright torso

exercises.name: Leg Press
exercises.notes: Feet shoulder width, don't lock knees at top

exercises.name: Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Hamstring isolation — slow negative

exercises.name: Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Full range — pause at bottom stretch and top squeeze

cooldown.description: 5 min quad + hamstring + hip flexor stretch

name: Bodyweight Baseline Circuit
description: The bare minimum. 20 minutes, no equipment, covers all major patterns. For athletes who just want to not get hurt.

main_set.description: Full body bodyweight circuit — do all exercises as a circuit, rest 60s between rounds

exercises.name: Push-Up
exercises.notes: Knees OK for beginners — full range of motion

exercises.name: Air Squat
exercises.notes: Hips below parallel if possible

exercises.name: Plank
exercises.notes: Straight line from head to heels

exercises.name: Reverse Lunge
exercises.notes: Step back, knee hovers above floor

exercises.name: Glute Bridge
exercises.notes: Squeeze at top for 2 sec

name: Runner's Minimum Effective Dose
description: The 4 exercises every runner should do, even if they do nothing else. 15 minutes, huge ROI.

warmup.description: 2 min easy jog in place + leg swings

main_set.description: The non-negotiable 4 for runners — glutes, hips, core, calves

exercises.name: Single-Leg Glute Bridge
exercises.notes: The most important exercise for runners — glute activation prevents IT band issues

exercises.name: Side-Lying Clamshell
exercises.notes: Hip abductor strength — prevents knee cave

exercises.name: Dead Bug
exercises.notes: Core stability under movement — protects the spine

exercises.name: Single-Leg Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Full range — prevents Achilles and plantar fasciitis
